title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: Q-matrix binary matrix specifies mapping item respective latent construct(s). , helps us define BFI items belong domain. nice feature representation allows easy computation sum scores domain matrix multiplication.
code: Q <- matrix(0, nrow = I, ncol = D)  rownames(Q) <- colnames(bfi) colnames(Q) <- c("a", "c", "e", "n", "o")  Q[1:5, "a"] <- 1 # Extraversion Q[6:10, "c"] <- 1 # Agreeableness Q[11:15, "e"] <- 1 # Conscientiousness Q[16:20, "n"] <- 1 # Neuroticism Q[21:25, "o"] <- 1 # Openness  # Sumscores (original) bfi %*% Q |> head() #>     a  c  e  n  o #> p1 17 16 17 14 19 #> p2 18 20 15 19 16 #> p3 22 20 19 18 18 #> p4 24 21 20 14 18 #> p5 17 18 18 16 16 #> p6 28 22 20 15 19

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: now, let’s focus Extraversion items:

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: happens, greadily drop, say 2 worst-performing items extraversion scale based alpha values? dropit() function allows us explore question.
code: drop2ga <- dropit(   extra,   n_drop = 2L,   direction = "tail",   criterion = "alpha",   approach = "greedy",   output_type = "both",   alpha_metric = c("raw_alpha"),   alpha_args = list(check.keys = TRUE),   verbose = FALSE ) drop2ga$names #> [1] "E2" "E4"  # Manifest correlation between sum scores cor(rowSums(extra), rowSums(drop2ga$subset), use = "pairwise.complete.obs") #> [1] 0.8282344

title: Authors and Citation

title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {dropit: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation},   author = {Steven Bißantz},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://sbissantz.github.io/dropit/}, }

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: dropit package provides tools reproducible psychometric experiments explore structural modifications item-based measurements affect outcome metrics. core function dropit() systematically removes items questionnaire data examine impact Cronbach’s alpha confirmatory factor analysis (CFA) loadings. procedures fully traceable, strict input validation, detailed message handling, informative console output support transparent iterative research workflows.

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: dropit package available CRAN. can install development version GitHub. Using pak (recommended): Alternatively, using remotes:

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: Contributions, suggestions, bug reports welcome. Please open issue submit pull request GitHub.

title: Warn About Ignored Arguments — check_ignored
text: Internal helper used emit message user-specified arguments applicable current method (e.g., alpha vs. lambda mode).

title: Warn About Ignored Arguments — check_ignored
Warn About Ignored Arguments — check_ignored
text: user_supplied Character vector argument names explicitly supplied user. ignored_names Character vector argument names applicable current context.

title: Warn About Ignored Arguments — check_ignored
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect printing message.

title: Warn About Ignored Arguments — check_ignored
text: function prints informative message rather warning, indicating arguments ignored intentionally.

title: Warn About Ignored Arguments — check_ignored
code: check_ignored(c("alpha_args", "lambda_metric"), c("lambda_metric")) #> Error in check_ignored(c("alpha_args", "lambda_metric"), c("lambda_metric")): could not find function "check_ignored" # Prints: Argument(s) 'lambda_metric' not applicable and ignored.

what: Print Colored Message to Console — colormsg
title: Print Colored Message to Console — colormsg
text: Prints message console using ANSI color codes. Supports standard 16-color extended 256-color modes, well bold styling.

title: Print Colored Message to Console — colormsg
code: colormsg(txt, color_code = 32, bold = FALSE, newline = FALSE)

title: Print Colored Message to Console — colormsg
text: txt Character scalar. message text print. color_code Integer character. ANSI color code (e.g., 31 = red, 32 = green, 33 = yellow, "38;5;244" = gray256). Defaults 32 (green). bold Logical; TRUE, applies bold formatting. Default FALSE. newline Logical; TRUE, prints newline message. Default FALSE.

title: Print Colored Message to Console — colormsg
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect printing colored text console.

title: Print Colored Message to Console — colormsg
code: colormsg("Hello World!", color_code = 31) #> Error in colormsg("Hello World!", color_code = 31): could not find function "colormsg" colormsg("Soft gray header", color_code = "38;5;245", bold = TRUE) #> Error in colormsg("Soft gray header", color_code = "38;5;245", bold = TRUE): could not find function "colormsg"

what: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
title: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
text: Low-level utilities used internally dropit() implement different item–removal strategies. functions intended direct use end-users documented developers may wish extend debug algorithm.

title: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
code: greedydrop_lambda(dta, n_drp, dir, out, mmt_mdl, tgt_fct, lam_mtr, cfa_args)  greedydrop_alpha(dta, n_drp, dir, out, alp_mtr, alp_args)  oneshotdrop_lambda(dta, n_drp, dir, out, mmt_mdl, tgt_fct, lam_mtr, cfa_args)  oneshotdrop_alpha(dta, n_drp, dir, out, alp_mtr, alp_args)

title: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
text: dta data.frame item responses (rows = respondents, columns = items). n_drp Integer scalar giving number items remove. dir Character string, either "tail" "head", passed utils::tail() / utils::head() select weakest strongest items. Character string, one "names", "subset", "", determining type object returned. mmt_mdl Character string lavaan model syntax CFA. NULL, single-factor model created automatically. tgt_fct Character scalar giving name target latent factor used ranking.  Required fitted CFA multiple factors. lam_mtr Character string passed lavaan::inspect() selecting standardized solution matrix extract (e.g., "std"). cfa_args Named list additional arguments passed lavaan::cfa(). alp_mtr Character string naming column psych::alpha$alpha.drop used ranking. alp_args Named list additional arguments passed psych::alpha(). crt Character string, "alpha" "lambda", indicating ranking criterion. apr Character string, "oneshot" "greedy", selecting removal strategy.

title: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
text: Depending : "names" – character vector dropped item names. "subset" – reduced data.frame dropped items removed. "" – list elements names subset.

title: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
text: Alpha methods call psych::alpha() check.keys = TRUE. Lambda methods call lavaan::cfa() (defaulting std.lv = TRUE unless overridden) extract "lambda" matrix lavaan::inspect(). helpers validate number extracted loadings matches number columns dta throw error otherwise.

title: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
text: naivedrop() Dispatch wrapper calls correct algorithm based criterion ("alpha" "lambda") approach ("oneshot" "greedy"). greedydrop_alpha() Iterative (“greedy”) removal using Cronbach’s alpha. oneshotdrop_alpha() Single–pass removal using Cronbach’s alpha. greedydrop_lambda() Iterative (“greedy”) removal using CFA factor loadings. oneshotdrop_lambda() Single–pass removal using CFA factor loadings.

what: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
text: Removes weakest (strongest) items psychometric scale either () minimizing decrease Cronbach’s alpha (b) selecting smallest absolute CFA loadings. function can operate full data set within user-defined column partitions.

title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
code: dropit(   data = data.frame(),   partition = NULL,   n_drop = 1L,   direction = c("tail", "head"),   criterion = c("alpha", "lambda"),   approach = c("oneshot", "greedy"),   output_type = c("names", "subset", "both", "debug"),   alpha_metric = c("raw_alpha", "std.alpha", "G6(smc)", "average_r", "S/N", "alpha se",     "var.r", "med.r"),   alpha_args = list(),   measurement_model = NULL,   target_factor = NULL,   lambda_metric = c("est", "std", "std.lv", "std.nox", "std.all"),   cfa_args = list(),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
text: data data.frame item responses (rows = respondents, columns = items). Must least one row one column. Non-data.frame inputs coerced. partition Optional character vector length ncol(data) giving partition label column. Dropping performed independently within partition. n_drop Integer scalar. Number items remove partition. direction Character string, "tail" (default) "head". "tail" drops weakest items; "head" drops strongest. criterion Character string, "alpha" (Cronbach’s alpha) "lambda" (CFA loadings). approach Character string, "oneshot" (single pass) "greedy" (iterative dropping refitting). output_type One "names" (default), "subset", "", "debug". "names" – character vector dropped item names. "subset" – reduced data frame dropped columns removed. "" – list elements names subset. "debug" – "" also includes captured messages, warnings, errors. alpha_metric Character string. Cronbach’s alpha metric optimise (passed psych::alpha$alpha.drop). alpha_args Named list extra arguments alpha. measurement_model Optional character string containing model.syntax specification. NULL, single-factor model items loading one latent factor used. target_factor Character scalar giving name factor whose loadings determine item ranking multiple factors present. lambda_metric Character string indicating solution matrix extract inspect (e.g., "est", "std", "std.lv", "std.nox", "std."). cfa_args Named list additional arguments passed cfa. verbose Logical; TRUE (default) prints structured, color-formatted report messages, warnings, errors captured run.

title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
text: output_type != "debug", returns either character vector, data frame, list depending specified output type. output_type = "debug", returns list components: result main result (NULL error occurred). warnings Character vector captured warnings. messages Character vector informational messages.

title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
text: Input validation strict: missing duplicated column names fixed rejected, partition must contain least n_drop items. Arguments applicable chosen method (e.g., alpha_args criterion = "lambda") detected automatically reported via informational message. messages, warnings, errors downstream calls captured printed together end verbose = TRUE, formatted using internal helper colormsg().

title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
text: Alpha – ranks items using alpha, dropping whose removal least reduces Cronbach’s alpha (measure internal consistency equals true reliability essential tau-equivalence holds). Lambda – fits one-factor CFA via cfa ranks items absolute standardized loadings.

title: Internal Utility Functions — miscutils
text: collection internal helper functions used across package message formatting, console output, argument checking.

title: Internal Utility Functions — miscutils
text: utilities exported primarily intended internal use developer-facing code dropit(). consoles (e.g., RGui Windows) may support ANSI colors. full color compatibility, use RStudio, VS Code, Unix terminal.

title: Internal Utility Functions — miscutils
text: colormsg() Prints colored optionally bold messages console using ANSI escape codes. trim_newlines() Removes leading trailing newline characters string. check_ignored() Emits informational message arguments applicable current method provided user.

what: Trim Leading and Trailing Newlines — trim_newlines
title: Trim Leading and Trailing Newlines — trim_newlines
text: Removes leading trailing newline (\\n) characters character string. Useful cleaning collected messages warnings printing.

title: Trim Leading and Trailing Newlines — trim_newlines
Trim Leading and Trailing Newlines — trim_newlines
text: x Character vector scalar process.

title: Trim Leading and Trailing Newlines — trim_newlines
text: character vector newlines removed start end.

title: Trim Leading and Trailing Newlines — trim_newlines
code:
trim_newlines("

Hello World!

") #> Error in trim_newlines("

Hello World!

"): could not find function "trim_newlines" # Returns "Hello World!"
